blocks.text: Wudu is more than just a physical cleansing; it is a spiritual reawakening. As each part of the body is washed, the believer is reminded of the mercy of Allah and the opportunity to wash away the traces of the world before standing in prayer. The Prophet ﷺ taught us that when a believer performs wudu, their sins fall away with the last drop of water. This ritual is the key to the prayer, and its proper performance is the first step toward achieving true Khushu.

blocks.textUthmani: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓاْ إِذَا قُمۡتُمۡ إِلَى ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ فَٱغۡسِلُواْ وُجُوهَكُمۡ وَأَيۡدِيَكُمۡ إِلَى ٱلۡمَرَافِقِ وَٱمۡسَحُواْ بِرُءُوسِكُمۡ وَأَرۡجُلَكُمۡ إِلَى ٱلۡكَعۡبَيۡنِۚ وَإِن كُنتُمۡ جُنُبٗا فَٱطَّهَّرُواْۚ وَإِن كُنتُم مَّرۡضَىٰٓ أَوۡ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ أَوۡ جَآءَ أَحَدٞ مِّنكُم مِّنَ ٱلۡغَآئِطِ أَوۡ لَٰمَسۡتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فَلَمۡ تَجِدُواْ مَآءٗ فَتَيَمَّمُواْ صَعِيدٗا طَيِّبٗا فَٱمۡسَحُواْ بِوُجُوهِكُمۡ وَأَيۡدِيكُم مِّنۡهُۚ مَا يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ لِيَجۡعَلَ عَلَيۡكُم مِّنۡ حَرَجٖ وَلَٰكِن يُرِيدُ لِيُطَهِّرَكُمۡ وَلِيُتِمَّ نِعۡمَتَهُۥ عَلَيۡكُمۡ لَعَلَّكُمۡ تَشۡكُرُونَ
blocks.translationEn: You who believe, when you are about to pray, wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, wipe your heads, wash your feet up to the ankles and, if required, wash your whole body. If any of you is sick or on a journey, or has just relieved himself, or had intimate contact with a woman, and can find no water, then take some clean sand and wipe your face and hands with it. God does not wish to place any burden on you: He only wishes to cleanse you and perfect His blessing on you, so that you may be thankful

blocks.text: This verse is the foundational command for Wudu, explicitly naming the four obligatory (Fardh) parts: washing the face, washing the arms up to the elbows, wiping the head, and washing the feet up to the ankles. While the Sunnah adds further beautiful details, these four are the absolute essentials without which the Wudu is not valid.

Start with a sincere intention (Niyyah) in your heart. Begin by saying 'Bismillah'. Wash your hands three times, ensuring water reaches between the fingers. Rinse your mouth thoroughly three times, then sniff water into your nose three times and blow it out with your left hand. These steps clear the senses and prepare you for the focused recitation of the prayer.

Wash your entire face three times, from the hairline to the chin and from ear to ear. Next, wash your right arm up to and including the elbow three times, then do the same for the left arm. This stage of Wudu involves the limbs we use most in our daily lives, symbolically purifying our actions before we stand in worship.

Wipe your wet hands over your entire head once, from front to back and back to front. Use your index fingers for the inside of your ears and thumbs for the back. Finally, wash your right foot including the ankle three times, ensuring you wash between the toes, then repeat for the left foot. This final step completes your physical preparation for the sacred meeting with your Lord.

blocks.textEn: Humran, the freed slave of 'Uthman, said:Uthman b. 'Affan called for ablution water and this is how he performed the ablution. He washed his hands thrice. He then rinsed his mouth and cleaned his nose with water (three times). He then washed his face three times, then washed his right arm up to the elbow three times, then washed his left arm like that, then wiped his head; then washed his right foot up to the ankle three times, then washed his left foot like that, and then said: I saw the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) perform ablution like this ablution of mine. Then the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: He who performs ablution like this ablution of mine and then stood up (for prayer) and offered two rak'ahs of prayer without allowing his thoughts to be distracted, all his previous sins are expiated. Ibn Shihab said: Our scholars remarked: This is the most complete of the ablutions performed for prayer

blocks.text: The Prophet ﷺ taught that whoever performs Wudu perfectly and then recites the Shahadah, the eight gates of Paradise are opened for them. This immense reward shows that Wudu is not a burden, but a blessed opportunity for grace and a re-affirmation of one's faith.

Be mindful of water usage. The Prophet ﷺ would perform Wudu with a very small amount of water. Rushing and wasting water are both discouraged; instead, focus on the quality and presence of heart in each movement of the Wudu.

blocks.textEn: Narrated AbuHurayrah: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: The prayer of a person who does not perform ablution is not valid, and the ablution of a person who does not mention the name of Allah (in the beginning) is not valid

blocks.text: This hadith emphasizes the importance of the initial 'Bismillah'. Mentioning the name of Allah at the start of Wudu connects the physical act to its spiritual purpose and brings barakah (blessing) to the entire process of purification.

As you finish your Wudu, feel the tranquility of being ritually pure. This state of Tahara is the armor of the believer and the bridge to a prayer filled with Khushu. You are now ready to stand before Allah with a clean body and a focused heart.
